mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals maximum monetary accommodation. Under this regime, equity valuations expand due to compressed discount rates and reduced opportunity cost of equity capital relative to cash. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of 2015-01-15 indicates zero lower bound regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window during zero-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) above 200k signals robust labor market expansion and rising consumer purchasing power. This drives aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations upward.
failure_condition: Positive payroll surprises (PAYEMS growth > 200k month-over-month) fail to produce positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer and construction demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, this indicates broad-based demand expansion that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 342,309M and HOUST at 582k as of 2015-01-15
regime_hint: synchronized demand recovery across consumer and construction sectors
failure_condition: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in both RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 52% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) decline of more than 0.5% month-over-month signals supply-side contraction and potential economic weakness. This triggers negative equity returns over 20 trading days as markets reprice earnings expectations downward and increase risk premiums.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO declines > 0.5% fail to predict negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as markets anticipate lower corporate earnings from reduced consumer spending and potential recession risk.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: 3-month UNRATE increases > 0.3pp fail to predict negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) year-over-year growth below 1.5% combined with FEDFUNDS near zero indicates deflationary pressure and monetary accommodation. This regime supports equity valuations through low real discount rates and reduced inflation risk premium, producing positive 20-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 214.47 and FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of 2015-01-15
regime_hint: low inflation with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: Simultaneous low inflation (CPIAUCSL YoY < 1.5%) and FEDFUNDS < 0.25% fail to produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 600k units annualized signals strong residential construction demand and forward-looking economic optimism. This produces positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as markets interpret housing strength as a leading indicator of sustained economic expansion.
regime_hint: housing market recovery and construction expansion
failure_condition: HOUST > 600k fails to predict positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth signals synchronized supply and labor expansion. This joint signal indicates broad-based economic momentum that supports equity valuations over 60 trading days through higher expected corporate earnings and productivity gains.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 95.97 and PAYEMS at 131,488k as of 2015-01-15
failure_condition: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in both INDPRO and PAYEMS fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 54% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 3-year window.
